title: Cotoneaster globosus
intro: Cotoneaster globosus, also known as the globous cotoneaster, is a deciduous shrub native to China, characterized by its rounded shape and clusters of white flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throat. Bioactive constituents of Cotoneaster globosus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cotoneaster globosus include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote respiratory health and relieve symptoms of upper respiratory tract infections.
taxonomy: Cotoneaster globosus is classified within the family Rosaceae. This family also includes other flowering plants such as roses and apples. Within the genus Cotoneaster, it is placed alongside other shrubs and small trees native to mountainous regions. The specific classification of Cotoneaster globosus is as a species of flowering plant in the genus Cotoneaster and family Rosaceae. Its classification reflects its relationships to other plants within the same family and genus.
uses: Cotoneaster globosus is a shrub native to China, but information about its medicinal uses is limited. However, it is known to produce berries and leaves that have been used in traditional Chinese medicine. In some cases, the berries and leaves may be used to treat fever, inflammation, and other health issues. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Cotoneaster globosus for medicinal purposes. The plant's potential medicinal uses should be explored under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
benefits: Cotoneaster globosus is known to have potential health benefits due to its rich content of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds. The plant's berries are particularly rich in anthocyanins and other polyphenols that have been linked to various health benefits. These compounds may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Additionally, the plant's leaves and stems contain flavonoids and other phenolic acids that may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and to establish its safety for human consumption.

side_effects: Cotoneaster globosus has not been extensively studied for its potential side effects, but like other members of the Rosaceae family, it may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. The plant's berries are also toxic to humans and animals if ingested, containing sorbitol, malic acid, and other compounds that can cause gastrointestinal upset. Ingestion of the berries may also lead to diarrhea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of Cotoneaster globosus.
